question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting a large volume of on-chain transactions that create millions of distinct accounts, cause the BALANCE_QUERY ('select account_id, balance from account_balance where consensus_timestamp = ?') to return an unbounded result set that is loaded entirely into a HashMap with no size cap, exhausting JVM heap memory and crashing the importer service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user, by creating millions of token associations across many accounts via normal token transactions, cause TOKEN_BALANCE_QUERY ('select account_id, token_id, balance from token_balance where consensus_timestamp = ?') to return an unbounded result set loaded into a HashMap, exhausting heap memory and causing an OutOfMemoryError that halts the reconciliation service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting a massive number of token transfer transactions between two balance file timestamps, cause TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY to return millions of rows loaded into the tokenBalances HashMap via Math::addExact, exhausting heap memory and causing a non-network DoS of the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting many large-amount crypto transfers to the same account such that the sum of amounts exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE, trigger an ArithmeticException from Math::addExact inside the transfersBalance.merge() call, causing the reconcile() method to catch it as FAILURE_UNKNOWN and permanently halt reconciliation progress?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting many large-amount token transfers to the same token-account pair such that the sum overflows a signed 64-bit long, trigger an ArithmeticException from Math::addExact inside tokenBalances.merge(), causing the outer catch block to set status to FAILURE_UNKNOWN and permanently block reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing a balance mismatch across millions of accounts, cause Maps.difference(transfersBalance, currentBalances) to construct an in-memory MapDifference object containing millions of entries, exhausting heap memory and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing a large balance mismatch, cause String.format(failureStatus.getMessage(), fromTimestamp, toTimestamp, difference) to call difference.toString() on a MapDifference with millions of entries, producing a string of gigabytes in size that exhausts heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation loop to process a very large number of balance files each containing millions of accounts, hold the synchronized lock on reconcile() indefinitely, preventing any other scheduled invocation from executing and causing a permanent DoS of the reconciliation service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by flooding the network with transactions that generate thousands of balance files, cause the while (current.isPresent()) loop to run for an extremely long time, holding the synchronized lock and blocking all subsequent scheduled reconciliation runs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions that cause the reconciliation job to enter an error state with ACCUMULATE strategy, cause the error field of ReconciliationJob to grow without bound via repeated StringUtils.joinWith('\\n', ...) calls across thousands of balance file iterations, eventually causing a DB column overflow or OOM when saving the job?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions that create accounts with balance values that sum to exactly FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS but include a very large number of accounts (e.g., tens of millions), cause the BALANCE_QUERY to return tens of millions of rows that are all loaded into a HashMap, exhausting heap memory before the fifty-billion check is even reached?
